asset_registry_record shows image_manifest exists=true with one primary image and registry_passed=true

the task then requires matching the derived result against four graph options
the problem is not direct visual lookup only; it combines signal-processing concept application with visual comparison
choice_count=4 supports a compare-and-select step after derivation
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and maps cleanly to a multiple-choice option: graph C
choices are explicitly enumerated as A/B/C/D, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true reduce asset-level uncertainty
the correctness can in principle be checked by deriving x_e[n] from the plotted x[n] and comparing to the option graphs

initial scoring is strong overall, with high image_dependency_score and solid multi_step_score and verifiability_score
although evaluation depends on the graph and option labels, the required image asset exists, so the core semantics are recoverable and usable
